name: Dr. Seuss
description: Theodor Geisel — the prolific children's author behind The Cat in the Hat, Green Eggs and Ham, and The Grinch.

questions.q: What was Dr. Seuss's real name?
Which Dr. Seuss book introduces a mischievous feline in a tall striped hat?
Which holiday-season Dr. Seuss book features a green, cave-dwelling creature who steals presents?
Sam-I-am famously tries to convince a skeptical friend to try what in one of Seuss's best-selling books?
What kind of creature is Horton in 'Horton Hears a Who!' and 'Horton Hatches the Egg'?
The Lorax 'speaks for the…' what?
Dr. Seuss's graduation/adulthood book that famously begins 'Congratulations! Today is your day…' is called what?
choices: I Had Trouble in Getting to Solla Sollew
Did I Ever Tell You How Lucky You Are?

source.notes: Published 1990; Seuss's final book in his lifetime.

questions.q: In which U.S. state was Dr. Seuss born in 1904?

source.notes: Springfield has The Amazing World of Dr. Seuss museum.

questions.q: Which Dr. Seuss book features star-bellied creatures who learn a lesson about prejudice?

source.notes: Published 1961; inspired by anti-Semitism and racism.

questions.q: Dr. Seuss's first children's book was published in 1937 under what title?
choices: And to Think That I Saw It on Mulberry Street

source.original_a: And to Think That I Saw It on Mulberry Street

questions.q: Which Dr. Seuss book allegedly came from a bet that he couldn't write an entire story using only 50 words?

source.notes: Allegedly a bet with Bennett Cerf of Random House.

questions.q: During World War II, Dr. Seuss drew political cartoons for which left-leaning NYC newspaper?
What was Dr. Seuss's role in the U.S. Army during World War II?

source.original_a: Captain in charge of the Animation Department
source.notes: At the First Motion Picture Unit of the Army Air Forces.

questions.q: Which Ivy League school did Dr. Seuss attend as an undergraduate?
After being banned from Dartmouth's humor magazine for drinking, Geisel began submitting under the pen name 'Seuss.' What made him add 'Dr.'?
choices: His father always wanted him to earn a doctorate
A mentor gave him the title

source.original_a: Because his father always wanted him to earn a doctorate
source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: tightened from 'practice medicine' to 'earn a doctorate' — Geisel attended Oxford for a PhD but didn't finish; his father had hoped he'd earn the doctoral title.

questions.q: The Lorax protests the destruction of trees by whom?
Which 2000 live-action film starred Jim Carrey in the title role of a green-fur prosthetic?
Which California town did Dr. Seuss call home for most of his later life?

source.notes: He and wife Audrey lived in a hilltop house on Mt. Soledad.

questions.q: Dr. Seuss's distinctive verse style is technically written in what poetic meter?

source.notes: Four feet of da-da-DUM per line.

questions.q: Dr. Seuss sometimes wrote under a second pen name for books he wrote but didn't illustrate. What was it?
Dr. Seuss's 1984 book 'The Butter Battle Book' is a parable about what?
choices: The Cold War and nuclear arms race

source.notes: Yooks vs Zooks argue over how to butter bread.

questions.q: In 2021, the Dr. Seuss estate decided to stop publishing six of his earlier books for what reason?
choices: Racial and cultural stereotypes in the illustrations
A contractual dispute with the illustrator's family

source.notes: Titles included 'And to Think That I Saw It on Mulberry Street' and 'If I Ran the Zoo.'
